data.title: School of Management Sciences
data.description: The School of Management Sciences (SMS) is one of the pioneering faculties at SIMAD University, focusing on developing specialized skills in the areas of management, business, marketing, entrepreneurship finance, and procurement and logistics. SMS prepares graduates who are well-educated, professional, and virtuous scholars in today’s modern world. The SMS offers a unique learning experience that takes place in a conducive environment generated by a team of dedicated School members and lecturers who are keen to develop the students’ natural desire for knowledge and equip them with the requisite skills to explore potential opportunities, contribute creative ideas, inspire peers, and navigate through the challenges of the modern business world. The graduates of SMS are trained to follow a wide variety of career paths, such as management, digital marketing, human resources, entrepreneurship, organizational development, procurement and logistics, and banking and financial management.

data.title: School of Accountancy
data.description: Because of the globalization of the world economy, industrialization, and technological developments of the country, the need for professional accountants with specialized skills such as accounting software packages and computer knowledge has steadily risen in Somalia. As a result, the university established the School of Accountancy (FA) in 2021, separating from the School of Management Sciences. FA was designed to provide professional-level accounting education to meet the growing public and private demands. The School seeks to promote knowledge and awareness of business principles and, more specifically, accounting principles, real-world perspectives, analytical and logical thinking, problem-solving, communication skills, and teamwork; foster excellence in teaching; acquire and disseminate accounting knowledge; and produce graduate in accounting who possess the attributes and skills that are needed to thrive in a technological and knowledge-based economy. Graduates from this School go into various careers, such as accountancy, banking, teaching, management, consultancy, industrial research and development, computer support, law, and many more.

data.title: School of Computing
data.description: The School of Computing (FC) is staffed by teaching professionals with an extensive variety of IT expertise and equipped with state-of-the-art learning and teaching aides & equipment. Innovation and creativity form the hallmark of the culture of the FC, as the information technology market demands innovators and creative problem solvers. We aspire to be the leading ICT training center in the Horn of Africa region. The FC has a variety of labs operating with the latest software and hardware systems. The need for computer and technology graduates with a broad range of skills such as internet-related technologies, e-commerce technologies, business data communication, software engineering, consultancy of information science, and programming knowledge is continuously rising across the globe. Somalia is no exception to these trends. The graduates of the School of Computing enter a wide variety of both private and public sectors of the economy.

data.title: School of Social Sciences
data.description: SIMAD University has established the School of Social Sciences to respond to the dynamics of the socio-political development and support Somalia's state-building initiatives. The aim was to effectively respond to the emerging and unfolding needs of the public service sector. The School of Social Sciences (FASS) is meant to serve as a source of knowledge for government employees and civil servants to support Somalia’s development and reconstruction processes. We serve the nation's development through knowledge sharing, innovation, and scientific research. Our programs are designed to provide real-world practical learning experiences that incorporate both hard and soft skills and to intellectually challenge with well-grounded theories in the social science field.

data.title: School of Economics
data.description: The School of Economics prepares students for a career in Economics, Statistics & Planning. Our unifying goal is to provide students with the skills, language, and tools to examine and contribute at a practical level to meaningful economic discussions locally and internationally. Our graduates will understand the economic decisions people, companies, and governments make to advance their interests. As one of the oldest and most well-established academic fields, Economics has robust and systematic analytical measures that allow economists to understand social behavior and attitudes from a unique angle, which makes it one of the most exciting fields of study. Students interested in pursuing a career in the areas of statistics and planning explore applied statistics through investigations in theoretical statistics as they apply to issues of planning and forecasting in the private and public sectors. The School has more than 15 distinguished, highly committed full-time professors with a proven record of accomplishment as researchers and educators. The School of Economics boasts state-of-the-art facilities and offers an excellent curriculum.

data.title: School of Education
data.description: Having a core of well-trained and qualified teachers is critical for the long-term prosperity of any country. A better teacher at both primary and secondary schools greatly enhances transition rates, resulting in more students entering universities. This is important not only for individual universities but also for national development. Therefore, SIMAD provides rigorous pre-service and in-service training for aspiring and practicing educators. As our core mission is to train the trainers, the graduates of the School of Education will contribute significantly to the development and capacity of teaching staff for primary and secondary schools.

data.title: School of Law
data.description:
Our School of Law was established in 2012 and tasked with the mission of providing legal education of the highest possible standard in quality to students in the First Cycle of studies meaning undergraduate consistent with the practice and promotion of Islamic principles and teachings in the legal profession and Somalia Educational formula utilized here at this university, The number of credit hours in the study plan for obtaining a bachelor’s degree in the School of Law is 183 credit hours.  The School includes a selection of the best lecturers specialized in law from various scientific ranks. The School contains teaching halls and equipped classes with modern teaching facilities, a library, and a Moot Court; the School pays special attention to scientific research and training for legal professionals. The School has distinguished relations with various state institutions, including ministries, unions, associations, government agencies, international and local agencies, and private sector companies. 

Furthermore, the School of law had a center called SIMAD Law Clinic Center for expanding the existing facilities, which were initially intended for the moot court, the special security issues, and other challenges that Somalia is going through. While the Law Clinic is essential to the well-being of the department and for the betterment, the department was established to task the following functions Moot Court, Legal Research, and Legal Aid.

data.title: School of Medicine and Health Sciences
data.description: Advancements in the field of medicine and the lack of qualified medical practitioners in Somalia dictated the need for competent doctors and graduates who could fulfill the existing medical needs of Somalia. As a result, the School of Medicine & Health Sciences (FMHS) was inaugurated in September 2015. The School is the first medical college in the country to integrate Problem-based learning (PBL) into its programs, offering students clinical scenarios that bridge the gap between theory and practice. The curriculum is oriented towards training students to undertake the responsibilities of a first-contact physician who is knowledgeable in preventive, curative, and rehabilitative aspects of medicine. The program ensures that our graduates have an excellent grasp of biological, psychological, and social mechanisms and processes and their impact on health and disease based on principles of learning drawn from cognitive and clinical psychology. The role of the FMHS is to contribute to the health and wellness of Somali society by training ethical and highly knowledgeable doctors.

data.title: School of Engineering
data.description: The School of Engineering (SE) aims to train qualified engineers who produce knowledge and technology for the benefit of Somali society and are sensitive to environmental and societal concerns. Faculties of Engineering graduates practice their profession at the highest standards with a strong sense of ethics. In addition to their qualification, the FE equips students with problem-solving, communication, teamwork, self-assessment, management, entrepreneurial, and lifelong learning skills. The FE curriculum has been designed to meet national needs and enable students to identify and solve complex engineering problems by choosing and applying appropriate methods. SE School members have obtained their degrees from respected universities in their field, and they are distinguished by research performance and commitment to excellence in teaching. The graduates of FE are qualified to embark on various careers and work in a wide range of industries in the public and private sectors.

data.title: OUM Programs
data.description: SIMAD University has a dynamic collaboration with Open University Malaysia, and are offering OUM postgraduate programs in Somalia since 2012. OUM is one of Malaysia's leading Online Distance Learning (ODL) universities.
